Stryker raises annual profit forecast on strong sales for medical devices
data.description:
Jan 22 - Medical equipment maker Stryker raised its full-year profit forecast on Thursday, banking on strong sales of its implants and other medical devices.

Shares of the company rose as much as 3% in extended trading.

The company, which makes joint replacements and medical implants to repair broken bones, now expects full-year earnings per share of $14.90 to $15.10, compared with its previous outlook of $13.50 to $13.60.

The upbeat forecast comes on the back of strong earnings in the fourth quarter, where Stryker posted earned $4.47 per share on an adjusted basis, compared with the average of analysts' estimates of $4.40 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.   

"Having surpassed $25 billion in (annual) revenue, we enter 2026 with significant momentum and are poised to continue delivering growth at the high end of MedTech," CEO Kevin A. Lobo said in a statement. 

Medical device makers have benefited from strong demand for surgical procedures in the last few quarters, but recent tariffs by U.S. President Donald Trump's administration have impacted the company.

In a post-earnings call, Lobo said he expects tariff impacts in 2026 to be about $400 million, which would be $200 million higher than the hit last year. The addition impact "will be realized in the first half of the year," Lobo added.

Stryker had previously said it planned to offset any tariff hit by optimizing its manufacturing footprint.

Sales at Stryker's medical surgery and neurotechnology unit rose 17.5% to $4.6 billion in the quarter, while its orthopedics segment saw a 2.2% increase in sales to $2.6 billion.

The Dublin-based company reported total revenue for the quarter ended December 31 of $7.17 billion, above analysts' expectations of $7.12 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

(Reporting by Bageshri Banerjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Alan Barona and Leroy Leo)

By Bageshri Banerjee

Deal reached advancing spending bills to avert US government agencies shutdown
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Jan 29 (Reuters) - A deal was reached on Thursday to advance a major package of spending bills that would avert government shutdowns of agencies beginning on Saturday, according to a Senate Democratic leadership aide.

The agreement also calls for splitting the Department of Homeland Security spending bill from the package and funding that agency for two-weeks at current levels.

(Reporting by Richard Cowan; Editing by Chris Reese)

Financials Up as Market Volatility Spurs Banks, Money Managers -- Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose as traders bet the sector would benefit from stable interest rates and hot financial markets.

Artificial intelligence stocks and precious metals saw wild swings Thursday -- volatility that's music to the ears of Wall Street traders.

Credit-card processor Mastercard said it plans to cut 4% of its roughly 35,000 employees and book a $200 million restructuring charge, becoming the latest in a series of companies that have announced job cuts in recent days.

President Donald Trump said he would unveil his nominee for the next chair of the Federal Reserve next week.

Fed Chairman Jerome Powell spoke about the importance of central-bank independence during a press conference following the Fed's policy statement.

One brokerage said economic data and the Fed board's confidence in those trends suggest rate cuts may be done for the foreseeable future, quelling fears about central-bank independence.

"We anticipate strong growth and sticky inflation, and regardless of who succeeds Powell, policy will likely be determined primarily by the economic outlook," economists at brokerage BNP Paribas Markets said in a note to clients.

Marsh's fourth-quarter net income rose amid revenue growth across the recently renamed firm's insurance and consulting brands.

First Brands Group founder Patrick James and his brother Edward were indicted by federal prosecutors on charges of defrauding lenders ahead of the auto-parts company's collapse into bankruptcy.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1752ET

data.title: Wall Street indexes see red as Big Tech's soaring AI budgets trigger flight
Elon Musk's SpaceX said to consider merger with Tesla, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Jan 29 (Reuters) - Elon Musk's SpaceX is considering a potential merger with Tesla, as well as an alternative combination with artificial-intelligence firm xAI, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Tesla's shares were up 3% after the bell following the report.

(Reporting by Juby Babu in Mexico City; Editing by Pooja Desai)
